articles.title: BITCOIN TO REBOUND PERFORM IN 2026, SAYS VANECK
articles.summary: VanEck's David Schassler predicts Bitcoin will be a "top performer" in 2026 after lagging the Nasdaq 100 by roughly 50% year-to-date, with the firm actively buying ahead of expected liquidity return and currency debasement acceleration. Gold surged 70% this year to $4,492, with Schassler targeting $5,000 in 2026 as funding future liabilities increasingly relies on money printing, pushing investors toward scarce stores of value. Bitcoin historically responds sharply when debasement ramps and liquidity returns, with the thesis intact despite 2025's weakness reflecting softer risk appetite and tight liquidity conditions.

articles.title: ‘MOST IMPORTANT TOKENHOLDER RIGHTS DEBATE': AAVE FACES IDENTITY CRISIS
articles.summary: Aave's community fractured over a proposal transferring brand control from Aave Labs to the DAO following CoW Swap fees flowing to Labs instead of the treasury, with the dispute centered on who controls trademarks, domains, and social accounts for the $33 billion TVL protocol. Labs supporters argue that company autonomy enables faster execution and competitive shipping, as "DAOs are structurally incapable" of product velocity, while DAO advocates led by Marc Zeller argue token holders bearing economic risk should control branding since independent service teams maintain the protocol. The vote currently shows 58% against transfer, with one-third abstaining ahead of Friday's conclusion, as other protocols watch with growing concern. AAVE fell 18% over the week as the dispute deepened beyond typical crypto market weakness.

articles.title: BROWSERBASE ENABLES AGENTIC PAYMENTS WITH COINBASE AND X402
articles.summary: Browserbase partnered with Coinbase to enable LLMs to discover services through x402 Bazaar and autonomously pay per browser session via instant USDC settlement, eliminating API keys, billing setup, and human approval for agent-driven web automation. Coinbase Business handles settlement with automatic yield on idle balances, institutional custody, and real-time visibility as agents spin up browsers, navigate live websites, and extract data programmatically. The integration unlocks workflows where agents use the web by interacting with real pages and interfaces while paying for compute autonomously without reconciliation work or fraud risk.

articles.title: GHANA TO EXPLORE GOLD-BACKED STABLECOIN
articles.summary: Ghana passed the Virtual Asset Service Providers Bill, legalizing crypto trading and requiring entities to register with the Bank of Ghana or Securities and Exchange Commission, depending on activity type, with Governor Johnson Asiama confirming "no one is going to be arrested" for trading under the new framework. The country processed $3 billion in crypto transactions from July 2023 to June 2024, with 17% of adults using crypto, positioning the legislation to manage risks in an already-active market. Ghana plans targeted exploration of asset-backed digital settlement instruments, including gold-backed stablecoins in 2026, focused on payments, trade finance, and FX settlement to lower costs, expand access, and strengthen compliance.

articles.title: ETHEREUM IN 2025: FROM EXPERIMENT TO WORLD INFRASTRUCTURE
articles.summary: 2025 was the year Ethereum graduated into real financial infrastructure. It experienced rapid protocol progress (e.g., Pectra and Fusaka, higher gas limits, cheaper transactions, and more blob capacity), plus institutional and regulatory milestones (stablecoin framework via GENIUS Act, SEC staking guidance, JPMorgan/BlackRock tokenization momentum). Ethereum's L2 ecosystem, DeFi, and privacy tooling have matured materially. AI-agent commerce rails (e.g., standards + micropayments) are also beginning to land on Ethereum.

articles.title: WHY THE DEFI LENDING MOAT IS BIGGER THAN YOU THINK
articles.summary: A common narrative is that vaults and curators own distribution, so they'll capture the economics. However, lending protocols (e.g., Aave and Spark) often earn more than the vaults built on top of them because they skim a reserve factor on interest paid on borrowed notional, while vault fees are usually charged on TVL. Aave and Spark capture multiples of vault revenue per unit of TVL and, in aggregate, even rival or beat upstream issuer economics (e.g., staked ETH issuers) in today's market structure.

articles.title: YIELDMAGGEDON: WHY LAZY YIELD IS DEAD; OPTIONS ARE THE NEW FRONTIER
articles.summary: The post argues that the easy, mostly delta-neutral 20-30% crypto yield era is largely over as the biggest historical yield sources (e.g., airdrops/points, perp incentives, basis trades, etc.) compress. With baseline yields (e.g., sUSDe) down sharply versus 2024, many yield products will either die or take hidden risks. The next frontier is onchain options selling (covered calls, call spreads, cash-secured puts) as a scalable way to earn yield by explicitly warehousing volatility rather than hunting temporary loopholes.

articles.title: TAO & WHAT IT FELT LIKE TO BE EARLY
articles.summary: Bittensor (TAO) is in the quiet infrastructure-building phase that preceded massive outcomes in Amazon, Bitcoin, Ethereum, Netflix, Tesla, and the market is mispricing it because it's complex, early, and noisy. Subnets are shipping quickly, there are early signs of real demand (a few subnets claiming paying customers and high performance), and improving market access (e.g., trust and ETP products).

articles.title: AAVE DAO VS AAVE LABS FIGHT
articles.summary: Aave's onchain protocol is owned and governed by the DAO, but a lot of the offchain items are controlled by Aave Labs.

articles.title: TOM LEE'S BITMINE ADDS ANOTHER $88M OF ETH TO GROWING TREASURY
articles.summary: BitMine reportedly bought 29,462 ETH ($88M) via BitGo and Kraken, though the specific transfers haven't been officially confirmed by BitMine.

articles.title: MAPLE'S FUNDAMENTALS ARE DRIVING OUTPERFORMANCE
articles.summary: SYRUP is outperforming other money markets, primarily driven by leading growth, yield, and revenue metrics.

articles.title: ETHZILLA SELLS $75M ETH
articles.summary: The ETHZilla DAT sold $74.5M ETH in a bid to grow its RWA tokenization business.
